findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:213 Daro already frames Sereya's act as necessary communal labor; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:225 Lale has already reduced the page to intention, action, and consequence; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:351 the scene acknowledges its own repetition; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:355 scene three then restates the same positions before the new external pressure arrives at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:403.
findings.problem: Once the Bitter Thread is underway, the chapter spends too long replaying interpretive positions the previous scene has already established. The pressure stays alive because the voices are good, but the structure stalls: the deck argument names its loop instead of producing a fresh leverage shift until the ankle-bell interruption arrives.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and re-stage the exchange around snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:301, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:333, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:351, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:373 so the scene moves from Daro pressing Nami to Nami's refusal at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:389 without replaying every sibling's full thesis. Keep one sharpened restatement at most, and replace the rest with a new behavioral consequence, confession, or work interruption before snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:403.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revision between snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:301 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:403 introduces at least one new leverage shift or material consequence before Daro restates his case, and no character repeats a thesis already made at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:213 or snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:225 unless that repetition visibly changes another character's behavior. FAIL if the scene still cycles through warning, evidence, bill, and meaning in sequence before Nami's line at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:389.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:27 uses 'No one answered her'; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:109 repeats 'No one answered'; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:241 repeats the same silence beat again; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:371 uses it a fourth time.
findings.problem: The chapter leans on the same null-response formula four times. In a chapter built around withheld reaction, that exact repetition stops reading as deliberate rhythm and starts reading as placeholder prose, flattening otherwise distinct moments of shock.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least two of the silence beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:371 with focalizer-specific physical or social reactions. The revised lines should show what the silence does to the current consciousness in each moment rather than naming silence in the same generic syntax every time.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, no more than two of the beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:27, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:109, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_10.md:371 still use 'No one answered' or an equivalent flat null-response construction, and each replacement is tied to a concrete focalizer-specific reaction. FAIL if three or more of those beats remain generic silence markers.

summary: Chapter 10 lands its mandated revelations, maintains close rotating interiority, and keeps the siblings' voices distinct under pressure. It still falls below pass level on craft and prose because scene three restages positions scene two has already established, and the repeated 'No one answered' beat becomes a noticeable chapter-level tic.
